positive_description: Left-lateralized mouth corner lift and cheek bunching. At positive values, the left side of the mouth pulls upward and outward while the right side stays relatively neutral. The effect is a left-sided smirk or half-smile. Subtle left-side nasolabial deepening accompanies the mouth movement.
negative_description: Left-lateralized mouth corner depression. The left mouth corner pulls slightly downward, creating a subtle asymmetric downturn. The left cheek flattens while the right side remains neutral.
symmetry_notes: Strongly asymmetric — this is one half of the psi11/psi12 conjugate pair. The deformation is lateralized to the left side of the face. The 3/4 views confirm the left-side bias: the left34 view shows more pronounced cheek/mouth movement than the right34 view.
artifact_notes: No visible mesh artifacts at +/-3.0. The asymmetric deformation is clean and well-formed. As a mid-frequency component, it stays within safe territory at normal weights.
primary_face_region: left mouth corner, left cheek, left nasolabial fold
useful_for: bilateral smile (as conjugate pair with psi12)

interactions.psi12: CONJUGATE PAIR. psi11+psi12 at equal weights produces bilateral smile. This is the primary smile mechanism used in current recipes.
interactions.psi9: psi9 provides a more symmetric full-face smile; psi11+psi12 adds a different smile character — more mouth-focused, knowing
interactions.psi1: psi1 is also asymmetric (lopsided smile) but as a primary component has much larger deformation per unit weight

current_recipes.role: Left mouth corner lift — half of the bilateral knowing smirk

prose: Psi11 is the left-handed member of the first asymmetric conjugate pair in the mid-frequency expression space. Applied alone, it produces a distinctly left-lateralized half-smile — the left mouth corner lifts while the right side stays put, creating a knowing smirk. Paired with its conjugate psi12 at equal weight, the two halves combine into a bilateral smile with a distinctive character: more mouth-focused and wry than the holistic smile of psi9. This conjugate pair is already the primary smile driver in the euphoric expression recipe, used at weight 2.5 each. The deformation is subtle compared to the primary components but clearly lateralized in the 3/4 views.
